fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Wisconsin Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Wisconsin - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity wisconsin, how to transfer occupational therapist license to wisconsin, occupational therapist compact wisconsin, wisconsin occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Wisconsin DSPS — Occupational Therapist
board.parent_dept: Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)
board.mailing_address:
Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services
P.O. Box 8368
Madison, WI 53708-8368

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Wisconsin.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create a DSPS LicensE Account
steps.description: Go to the DSPS LicensE portal (online.drl.wi.gov/Default.aspx) and register as a new user to create your online account.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your DSPS LicensE account, select 'Apply for a License,' and complete the Occupational Therapist application form, paying the required fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcript
steps.description: Have your occupational therapy program send official transcripts directly to the Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS) from your educational institution.

steps.title: Submit NBCOT Scores
steps.description: Arrange for the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) to send your official examination scores directly to DSPS.

steps.title: Provide Licensure Verification (if by Endorsement)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, request official verification of your current occupational therapy license(s) from any state(s) where you are currently or have been previously licensed, sent directly to DSPS.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Wisconsin offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Wisconsin DSPS — Occupational Therapist for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Wisconsin is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Wisconsin without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Wisconsin also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Wisconsin OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $78. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Wisconsin have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Wisconsin has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Wisconsin?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Wisconsin while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Wisconsin is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Wisconsin without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Wisconsin also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in Wisconsin
hero.dek: Occupational Therapists applying for licensure in Wisconsin must use the DSPS LicensE online system to submit their application.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: 1. Go to the DSPS LicensE portal. 2. Click on 'New User Registration' to create an account. 3. Follow the prompts to provide your email, create a password, and verify your account. 4. Once registered and logged in, select 'Apply for a License' to begin the Occupational Therapist application.
